text: {"type":"DRAG_FILL","title":"Building Foundations: Order C++ Statements","lessonText":"","data":{"challenges":[{"instruction":"Fill in the blanks with the correct C++ statements to declare and initialize variables.","codeTemplate":"int ___ = 10;\nfloat ___ = 3.14;\n___ = ___ + float;","options":["number","value","int","float","=","+"],"correctAnswer":"number, value, int, float","explanation":"Variable declaration reserves memory and sets the data type, while initialization assigns an initial value."},{"instruction":"Complete the code snippet to calculate the area of a circle using the formula area = π * radius^2.","codeTemplate":"float area = ___ * ___^___;","options":["π","radius","2","area","="],"correctAnswer":"π, radius, 2","explanation":"This challenge tests your understanding of variable declaration, initialization, and using operators to perform calculations."},{"instruction":"Write a C++ statement to declare and initialize an array of 5 integers with values from 1 to 5.","codeTemplate":"int ___[5] = {_, _, _, _, _};","options":["array","1","2","3","4","5"],"correctAnswer":"array, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5","explanation":"This challenge tests your understanding of declaring and initializing arrays in C++."},{"instruction":"Complete the code snippet to find the maximum of two numbers using a ternary operator.","codeTemplate":"int max = (___ > ___) ? ___ : ___;","options":["a","b","a","b","max"],"correctAnswer":"a, b, a, b","explanation":"This challenge tests your understanding of using ternary operators to make decisions in C++."}]},"references":[{"title":"Wikipedia Topic Overview","url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Search?search=C%2B%2B%20for%20Game%20Development%3A%20A%20Youth-Focused%20Course%20C%2B%2B%20Basics%3A%20Debunking%20Myths%20and%20Building%20Foundations%20Building%20Foundations%3A%20Order%20C%2B%2B%20Statements","kind":"web"},{"title":"Google Scholar Search","url":"https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=C%2B%2B%20for%20Game%20Development%3A%20A%20Youth-Focused%20Course%20C%2B%2B%20Basics%3A%20Debunking%20Myths%20and%20Building%20Foundations%20Building%20Foundations%3A%20Order%20C%2B%2B%20Statements","kind":"web"}]}
